AuroraTimes.Headline: Tesco Buyback Advances
AuroraTimes.Summary: Tesco bought back another 1.94m shares at an average 468.38p, taking total repurchases since April to 70.6m shares worth ?322.5m. Fewer shares in issue can support earnings per share, dividend-per-share growth and long-term shareholder returns.

AuroraTimes.Headline: UK Inflation Holds at 2.8%
AuroraTimes.Summary: UK inflation came in at 2.80%, below the 3.00% forecast and 2.93% community expectation. This is positive for the Aurora income portfolio because it reduces pressure on interest rates, which can help dividend shares, REITs and infrastructure funds, while petrol, transport and energy remain watch points.
AuroraTimes.Action_Required: Monitor the next CPI print and Bank of England rate path before changing allocations.
AuroraTimes.Notes: Actual 2.80 came in better than forecast/community expectations; supports current dividend-income stance.

AuroraTimes.Headline: Tesco Q1 Sales Grow; FY27 Profit Guidance Affirmed
AuroraTimes.Summary: Tesco reported Q1 sales of ?16.83bn for the 13 weeks ended 30 May, up 2.3% year over year at actual rates and 1.0% like-for-like. FY27 group adjusted operating profit guidance was reaffirmed at ?3.0bn??3.3bn.
AuroraTimes.Action_Required: No immediate action. Monitor Tesco price reaction and confirm full trading update when available.
AuroraTimes.Notes: Positive trading update: Q1 sales growth and FY27 adjusted operating profit guidance reaffirmed. Supports Tesco confidence but no purchase action needed today.

AuroraTimes.Headline: Payday Transfer Window Completed
AuroraTimes.Summary: Purchases completed: FSFL ?200 and RGL ?100 in Trade212, plus UKW ?49.32 and ARCC ?138.21 in IG ISA. Estimated annual income added is about ?51.89.
AuroraTimes.Action_Required: Holdings, Watchlist, Global Watchlist, LivePrices, IncomeLog, AuroraFixtures and dividend tracking reviewed.
AuroraTimes.Notes: Final corrected fills: FSFL 272.47956403 shares; RGL 108.10914350 shares; UKW 47 shares in IG ISA; ARCC 10 shares in IG ISA.

AuroraTimes.Headline: Regional REIT Leases Two Office Properties in England
AuroraTimes.Summary: Regional REIT leased two vacant office buildings in Nottingham to a specialist electronics manufacturer. The 20-year lease for One and Two Newstead Court covers 146,262 sq ft and is expected to generate ?1.1m of annual income, with inflation reviews every five years and break options after 10 and 15 years.
AuroraTimes.Action_Required: No immediate action. Positive lease news; monitor occupancy, income collection and property concentration.
AuroraTimes.Notes: Positive operational update for newly bought RGL holding. Supports confidence but does not change risk controls.

AuroraTimes.Headline: Bank of England Holds Interest Rates Steady
AuroraTimes.Summary: UK interest rates stayed unchanged at 4.25%, matching forecast and previous rate levels. This is neutral-to-positive for the Aurora income portfolio because it avoids fresh rate pressure on REITs, infrastructure funds and dividend shares, but it is not yet the stronger boost that a rate cut would provide.
AuroraTimes.Source: Interest rate screenshot / UK macro update
AuroraTimes.Action_Required: No immediate action. Keep current income stance and monitor the next Bank of England meeting for rate-cut signals.
AuroraTimes.Notes: Rates held steady: good for stability, but no extra rate-cut boost yet. Supports today?s income buys without changing risk controls.

AuroraTimes.Headline: Bernstein Tweaks Estimates for Tesco After Fiscal Q1 Update
AuroraTimes.Summary: Bernstein revised Tesco estimates after the fiscal Q1 update. UK like-for-like sales were softer than expected at 1.8%, but Bernstein views this as a temporary seasonal effect rather than deterioration in the underlying business. Tesco continues to gain market share, while fresh food, private label, premium private label and customer satisfaction are still improving. Q2 LFL forecasts were lowered, but EPS forecasts were lifted to ?0.31 from ?0.29 due to finance cost assumptions and the share buyback price. Bernstein rates Tesco outperform with a ?5.20 price target.
AuroraTimes.Action_Required: No immediate action. Keep holding; monitor Q2 like-for-like sales, non-food weakness, inflation/pricing pressure and ongoing buyback support.
AuroraTimes.Notes: Broker note is mildly positive overall: softer UK LFL and Q2 outlook are watch points, but market-share gains, EPS upgrade, buyback support, outperform rating and ?5.20 target support Tesco confidence.

AuroraTimes.Headline: Moody's Keeps M&G Rating at A3; Outlook Stable
AuroraTimes.Summary: Moody's affirmed M&G's long-term issuer rating at A3 with a stable outlook. The rating highlights solid and resilient capital adequacy, a substantial loss-absorbing buffer in the with-profits fund, sound asset quality, healthy financial flexibility and an improving franchise. The stable outlook reflects expectations that expansion plans and sizeable with-profit earnings will support profitability and earnings diversification.
AuroraTimes.Affected_Area: Financials / Core Income Holding / Credit Quality
AuroraTimes.Action_Required: No immediate action. Supports confidence in M&G as a high-income financial holding; keep payout-risk discipline and monitor future rating or capital updates.
AuroraTimes.Notes: Positive credit-quality update. This supports MNG board confidence but does not remove the need for payout and concentration checks in the DecisionEngine.

AuroraTimes.Headline: Supermarket Income REIT raises ?100m and agrees ?118m supermarket acquisition
AuroraTimes.Summary: SUPR raised ?100m gross at 83p per share through the issue of 120,481,928 new shares, about 10% of the pre-issue share capital. The proceeds, with leverage, support a nine-asset pipeline; the company also exchanged contracts to buy three supermarkets for ?118m at an average 6.9% net initial yield.
AuroraTimes.Source: Supermarket Income REIT RNS / London Stock Exchange
AuroraTimes.Action_Required: No sell action. Monitor dilution, acquisition completion, leverage and whether the enlarged portfolio improves dividend cover.
AuroraTimes.Notes: Material capital raise and acquisition. Does not match an enabled automatic SellRule.

AuroraTimes.Headline: Barratt Redrow reports FY26 profit in line and starts new buyback
AuroraTimes.Summary: Barratt Redrow said FY26 adjusted profit before tax was in line with market expectations after completing 17,667 homes in a challenging market. It also announced the commencement of a share buyback programme, increasing shareholder returns while demand and sector conditions remain subdued.
AuroraTimes.Action_Required: No immediate action. Keep on the transfer watchlist; assess full-year results and the sustainability of shareholder returns before promotion.
AuroraTimes.Notes: Material update for a top-five transfer target. No SellDesk or IncomingOffers action.

AuroraFixtures.Notes: Completed: FSFL ?200 and RGL ?100 in Trade212; UKW ?49.32 and ARCC ?138.21 in IG ISA. Total deployed ?487.53.

Milestones.Board_Message: Main club objective: reach the Champions League final.

AuroraRules.Notes: Shareholder return / fewer shares in issue.
Macro pressure; override impact if inflation is market-friendly.
Rate decisions can move income shares and REITs; override if result is favourable.
Target upgraded, shortlisted or moved to transfer watch.
Potential training/recovery event, not always bad if it creates value.
Recommendation must consider market regime before score/yield.
Block or caution buys if SectorLimits status is breached.
High/unsafe/check payout risk turns Buy into Caution or Monitor.
High yield is allowed only as small buy if concentration and payout gates pass.
Every no-buy or caution should show a reason, not just disappear.
Credit rating or capital strength update. Positive ratings support confidence, but do not override payout or concentration gates.
